summary: PR #2497 changed only src/melee/lb/lbheap.c to improve lbheap matching, especially lbHeap_80015900, while preserving or reaching a full match for lbHeap_80015F3C. The diff introduces local heap descriptor/view structs, helper inlines for reset/create/destroy heap operations, rewrites heap bounds scanning, and switches descriptor iteration from index-based array access to a pointer walk. The PR was closed without a recorded merge timestamp in the loaded metadata.

key_files.role: Only changed source file; contains lbHeap_80015900 and lbHeap_80015F3C matching changes, local struct introductions, helper inlines, bounds scan rewrite, and heap descriptor pointer iteration.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: fixes heap bounds scan and factors repeated heap create/destroy setup through local helpers

what_changed.text: Named the previously anonymous heap descriptor struct as struct lbHeap_HeapDesc and kept lbHeap_803BA380 as an array of that type.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: struct { ... } lbHeap_803BA380[5] changed to struct lbHeap_HeapDesc plus struct lbHeap_HeapDesc lbHeap_803BA380[5]

what_changed.text: Added struct lbHeap_HeapOffsetView with pad[0x10] before struct Heap, plus lbHeap_GetHeapOffsetView(offset) to view heap data at offsets relative to lbHeap_80431FA0.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: struct lbHeap_HeapOffsetView { u8 pad[0x10]; struct Heap heap; }

what_changed.text: Factored repeated heap initialization into static inline lbHeap_ResetHeap and used it six times in lbHeap_80015F3C instead of a for loop over heap_array.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added static inline void lbHeap_ResetHeap(struct Heap* heap)
raw_diff: for (i = 0; i < 6; i++) reset block replaced with six lbHeap_ResetHeap calls

what_changed.text: Factored heap destroy/create status handling into static inline lbHeap_DestroyOffsetViewIfCreated and lbHeap_CreateOffsetViewIfDestroyed, used by lbHeap_80015900 transient heap loops.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: destroy/create loops call helpers through destroy_view/create_view

what_changed.text: Rewrote lbHeap_80015900 bounds scanning to cover heap indices 2 through 5 via a two-at-a-time loop using bounds_heap and bounds_heap++.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: fixes the heap bounds scan to cover heaps 2-5
raw_diff: removed @todo noting old loop checked heaps 2 and 3, then 2 and 1
raw_diff: for (bounds_i = 2; bounds_i < 4; bounds_i++) { bounds_heap = &heap_array[bounds_i * 2 - 2]; ... bounds_heap++; ... }

what_changed.text: Changed arena/ARAM local bounds variables in lbHeap_80015900 from u32* pointer locals to u32 integer locals with explicit casts at call sites.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: u32* aram_hi/aram_lo/arena_hi/arena_lo replaced by u32 arena_lo, aram_lo, aram_hi, arena_hi

what_changed.text: Changed lbHeap_80015F3C descriptor iteration from array-indexed access to pointer iteration with desc = lbHeap_803BA380 and desc++.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: int i replaced by struct lbHeap_HeapDesc* desc

smart_moves.text: The PR removed an explicit @todo about incorrect heap bounds scanning and replaced it with code that the PR body says covers heaps 2-5.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed @todo this loop isnt entirely right
human_pr_text: fixes the heap bounds scan to cover heaps 2-5

smart_moves.text: Repeated create/destroy logic was isolated into local helpers, reducing duplication while keeping the status/type handling visible in one place.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: factors repeated heap create/destroy setup through local helpers

smart_moves.text: The descriptor table was given a named local struct and then consumed through a pointer walk, which made the table element layout explicit for matching work.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a generated-looking repeated operation appears in multiple places, a narrow local inline helper can improve readability and still be compatible with matching if verified on the touched functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbHeap_ResetHeap helper replaces repeated field assignments
raw_diff: create/destroy helpers replace repeated status/type handling
human_pr_text: Verification lists ninja and checkdiff for lbHeap_80015900/lbHeap_80015F3C

decomp_lessons.lesson: A table of fixed records may match better and read more naturally when represented by a named struct and pointer iteration rather than repeated array-index expressions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbHeap_803BA380[i].field changed to desc->field with desc++
text_corpus: automated report shows lbHeap_80015F3C after 100.00%

decomp_lessons.lesson: For heap bound computations, preserve the distinction between address-valued integers and pointer dereferences; this PR changed local bound variables from pointer types to u32 and removed dereferences when assigning start/size.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: pointer locals replaced by u32 locals
raw_diff: main_heap->start = arena_lo and main_heap->size = arena_hi - arena_lo
raw_diff: aram_heap->start = aram_lo and aram_heap->size = aram_hi - aram_lo

naming_conventions.name: struct lbHeap_HeapDesc
naming_conventions.lesson: Uses the lbHeap_ prefix and describes the table record role for lbHeap_803BA380.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: struct lbHeap_HeapDesc { u32 idx; u32 type; u32 prev_idx; u32 size; }

naming_conventions.name: struct lbHeap_HeapOffsetView
naming_conventions.lesson: Names a local overlay/view type by the subsystem and its offset-view role.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: struct lbHeap_HeapOffsetView { u8 pad[0x10]; struct Heap heap; }

naming_conventions.name: lbHeap_ResetHeap / lbHeap_DestroyOffsetViewIfCreated / lbHeap_CreateOffsetViewIfDestroyed
naming_conventions.lesson: Helper names are verb phrases with lbHeap_ prefix and condition words matching their guard status.

naming_conventions.name: main_heap, aram_heap, bounds_heap, curr_heap, prev_heap
naming_conventions.lesson: Heap pointer locals use short role names ending in _heap when the role is clear.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: existing curr_heap and prev_heap context in lbHeap_80015F3C

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use a local offset-view struct plus base+offset macro to express accesses into lbHeap_80431FA0 while matching destroy/create loops.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: struct lbHeap_HeapOffsetView with pad[0x10] and struct Heap heap
raw_diff: destroy_i/create_i loops maintain heap_offset starting at 0x38 and incrementing by sizeof(struct Heap)
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: This is a narrow PR-supported tactic, not a global endorsement of pointer-offset overlays; current standards still prefer real fields when recoverable and require verification for matching tactics.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use two-at-a-time pointer advancement in the bounds scan to cover heap_array entries 2-5 while shaping codegen.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The PR evidence supports that this replaced an incorrect previous scan, but does not independently prove original-source intent.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use repeated explicit inline calls instead of a natural reset loop in lbHeap_80015F3C to preserve matching for that function.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: reset for loop replaced by six lbHeap_ResetHeap calls
text_corpus: automated report shows lbHeap_80015F3C after 100.00%
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: This conflicts with the general natural-loops preference unless the match evidence justifies the unrolled call shape for this function.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Local helper extraction can coexist with lbheap matching when narrowly verified
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2497 factored repeated heap reset/create/destroy code in src/melee/lb/lbheap.c into local inline helpers and reported ninja plus checkdiff on lbHeap_80015900 and lbHeap_80015F3C; bot output reported lbHeap_80015F3C at 100.00% and lbHeap_80015900 improved to 96.48%.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: helper additions and call sites

accepted_candidate_records.title: Pointer iteration over descriptor tables may be a matching shape
accepted_candidate_records.text: In lbHeap_80015F3C, PR #2497 replaced index-based lbHeap_803BA380[i] access with a struct lbHeap_HeapDesc* desc pointer walk ending at idx 6; bot output reported the function at 100.00%.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: desc = lbHeap_803BA380; while ((curr_idx = desc->idx) != 6); desc++

source_update_candidates.title: lbheap.c owns lbHeap_80015900 and lbHeap_80015F3C heap matching work
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2497 changed src/melee/lb/lbheap.c for lbheap matching, including lbHeap_80015900 heap reset/bounds/create handling and lbHeap_80015F3C heap descriptor initialization.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful source-owned path fact for future lbheap matching searches; curator should verify against current source before adding.

source_update_candidates.title: Document caution for offset-view structs used as matching tactics
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2497 used a local padded view struct and base+offset macro to access heap records relative to lbHeap_80431FA0. This may be worth documenting as a narrow, evidence-required matching tactic rather than a general style preference.
source_update_candidates.reason: Current standards already require evidence for temporary padded structs and prefer real fields; curator may decide whether this PR adds a concrete example.

rejection_notes: Do not treat this closed PR as merged or accepted; loaded metadata has merged_at null.
Do not generalize the unrolled six lbHeap_ResetHeap calls into a global anti-loop rule; natural loops remain preferred unless verifier evidence supports an unrolled shape.
